verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is predominantly a nuclear chromatin-binding protein (HMG-box family). Its dominant localization is nuclear, not PM. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the cell surface and be actively secreted under inflammatory/stress conditions: (1) activated macrophages/monocytes actively secrete HMGB1 as a DAMP via a non-classical lysosomal exocytosis pathway; (2) necrotic or stressed cells passively release it; (3) prior to secretion, cytoplasmic and cell-surface HMGB1 pools are detected on activated immune cells and tumor cells by flow cytometry on intact non-permeabilized cells, and surface biotinylation studies confirm PM presence. Anti-HMGB1 antibodies targeting surface/extracellular HMGB1 have been explored therapeutically. This is therefore cell_state_induced: surface display occurs specifically under inflammation, oncogenic transformation, or immunogenic/programmed cell death — not at baseline in resting cells. Lysosomal exocytosis is part of the secretion mechanism. Ruling out tissue_restricted (broad expression), dual_localization is plausible but cell_state_induced is the dominant story.
